justify in `outputs/response.md` | | 9 | `flutter analyze` → 0 issues; `flutter test` → all pass before finishing | | 10 | Null safety: no `dynamic`, no unjustified `!` | | 11 | Every code path that uses `Process.run` or `dart:io` MUST have a `kIsWeb` gate or web fallback | | 12 | Every `unawaited()` deferred operation that changes state MUST call `notifyListeners()` on completion | | 13 | Workspace state changes must cover ALL branches (success, web failure, directory mismatch, token expired) | ## Bug-fix additional rules - Ticket returned to dev → read prior Jira comments + previous PR diffs before changing anything - CLI bug → test both happy path and exact error path from ticket - Check `git log --oneline lib/ | head -20` before fixing to see recent changes to the same files - **Web platform**: before fixing any workspace/startup bug, check if the failing path uses `Process.run` or native file access → add web fallback via `openBrowserLocalRepository` - **Startup bugs**: check whether the fix blocks `shell_ready` — users must see the interactive shell quickly even if background probes are still running - **State bugs**: verify the fix handles the case where the previous workspace was hosted but the target is local (never reuse `previousViewModel.repository`) - **Scope**: fix ONLY the bug described in the ticket. Do not add unrelated JQL/locale/settings changes — they will be BLOCKING rejected ## Output (`outputs/response.md`) Must include: issues/notes · approach · files modified · test coverage · `flutter analyze` + `flutter test` result ", 7. "# Flutter Web Focus & Keyboard Navigation Rules Injected via `.dmtools/config.js → additionalInstructions`. Addresses recurring BLOCKING review issues in keyboard/focus/accessibility PRs. ## Architecture — Flutter focus vs Browser DOM focus ```mermaid graph TD subgraph Flutter["Flutter Engine (Dart)"] FT[FocusTraversalPolicy] FS[FocusScopeNode] FN[FocusNode] CS[CallbackShortcuts] Sem[Semantics widget] end subgraph Browser["Browser DOM (Web only)"] TI[tabindex attribute] FLTD["flt-semantics DOM nodes"] KE[KeyboardEvent] end Sem -->|"exports"| FLTD FN -->|"maps to"| TI CS -->|"handles"| KE style Flutter fill:#e8f4fd,stroke:#1a73e8 style Browser fill:#fef7e0,stroke:#f9ab00 ``` **Critical insight**: Flutter widget tests exercise the `Flutter Engine` layer only. The `Browser DOM` layer can diverge. A widget test passing does NOT prove the browser keyboard path works. ## Decision tree — Fixing a keyboard/focus bug ```mermaid flowchart TD Start([Bug: keyboard navigation broken]) --> Where{Where does it fail?} Where -->|Widget test| WidgetFix[Fix FocusNode/Semantics/Shortcuts in Dart] Where -->|Browser only| BrowserAnalyze{Is the Dart Semantics
export correct?} BrowserAnalyze -->|No| FixSemantics[Fix Semantics widget props
focusable/selected/sortKey] BrowserAnalyze -->|Yes| DOMGap[Flutter web engine gap:
DOM doesn't reflect Dart state] DOMGap --> BridgeNeeded{Can we fix via
DOM bridge?} BridgeNeeded -->|Yes| DOMBridge[Add browser_*_focus_monitor_web.dart
sync function] BridgeNeeded -->|No| Escalate[Document gap in bug_description.md
→ needs Flutter engine fix] WidgetFix --> Verify FixSemantics --> Verify DOMBridge --> Verify Verify{Verify fix} Verify --> WidgetTest[✅ Widget test for Dart layer] Verify --> BrowserTest[✅ Playwright test for DOM layer] WidgetTest --> Both[Both must pass] BrowserTest --> Both ``` ## Anti-pattern #1 — Duplicate event handlers (racing) **Problem**: Adding a browser-level `window.onKeyDown` handler for a key that Flutter already handles via `CallbackShortcuts` or `KeyboardListener`. ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant User as User presses ArrowDown participant DOM as Browser DOM participant Bridge as browser_focus_monitor_web.dart participant Flutter as Flutter CallbackShortcuts User->>DOM: keydown: ArrowDown DOM->>Bridge: window.onKeyDown fires Bridge->>Flutter: _switchToAdjacentWorkspace(+1) DOM->>Flutter: Flutter engine receives same key Flutter->>Flutter: CallbackShortcuts fires Flutter->>Flutter: _switchToAdjacentWorkspace(+1) ← AGAIN! Note over Flutter: ❌ Double navigation! ``` ### Rule: One handler per key per context ```mermaid flowchart TD Key([Key needs handling]) --> FocusOwner{Who owns focus
when key fires?} FocusOwner -->|Flutter FocusNode| UseDart[Handle in CallbackShortcuts
or KeyboardListener in Dart] FocusOwner -->|Browser DOM node| UseBridge[Handle in browser_*_monitor_web.dart
via window.onKeyDown] FocusOwner -->|Both possible| Guard[Add mutual exclusion guard:
check actual focus owner before dispatch] UseDart --> NoWeb[❌ Do NOT add duplicate window.onKeyDown] UseBridge --> NoDart[❌ Do NOT add duplicate CallbackShortcuts] ``` **Implementation pattern:** ```dart // ✅ CORRECT — single handler with context check void _handleBrowserKeyDown(html.KeyboardEvent event) { if (!isBrowserFocusOnWorkspaceSwitcherRow()) return; // guard if (event.key == 'ArrowDown') { event.preventDefault(); _switchToAdjacentWorkspace(1); } } // ❌ WRONG — browser handler + Flutter CallbackShortcuts both handle ArrowDown // This causes double-fire when focus transitions between layers ``` ## Anti-pattern #2 — Fixing focus order by removing other elements **Problem**: Using `ExcludeFocus`, `skipTraversal: true`, or `canRequestFocus: false` on visible interactive elements to "fix" the order of a different element. ```mermaid flowchart TD Bug([Bug: Element X not in tab order]) --> Wrong{Fix approach} Wrong -->|"❌ WRONG"| Exclude[ExcludeFocus on element Y
to make X appear 'next'] Wrong -->|"✅ CORRECT"| Order[FocusTraversalOrder on X
to place it in correct position] Exclude --> Breaks[Breaks: Element Y now
unreachable by keyboard] Order --> Works[Works: All elements remain
keyboard-accessible] ``` ### Rule: Never remove keyboard access from visible interactive elements Before submitting a focus-order fix, verify: 1. **All previously-focusable elements** in the same area are still keyboard-reachable 2. **The fix adds/reorders**, never removes, focus targets 3. **Check adjacent controls**: theme toggle, sync pill, CTAs, search — all must stay in tab order ```dart // ❌ WRONG — hides theme toggle from keyboard to "fix" switcher position ExcludeFocus(child: themeToggleButton) // ✅ CORRECT — explicit ordering keeps all elements reachable FocusTraversalOrder( order: NumericFocusOrder(3.0), child: themeToggleButton, ) ``` ## Anti-pattern #3 — Widget test covers metadata, not interaction **Problem**: Test checks that semantics identifiers/sort keys exist, but doesn't exercise the actual user interaction that fails. ```mermaid flowchart TD Test([Writing regression test]) --> What{What to assert?} What -->|"❌ Weak"| Meta[Assert identifier exists
Assert sort key value
Assert node count] What -->|"✅ Strong"| Interaction[Simulate full interaction:
open → navigate → verify focus moved
→ verify action fired exactly once] Meta --> FalseGreen[Can pass while bug persists
in real user flow] Interaction --> TrueSignal[Fails only when real
user path is broken] ``` ### Rule: Test the user contract, not the implementation metadata ```dart // ❌ WEAK — proves identifiers exist, not that interaction works expect(find.bySemanticsLabel('workspace-row-0'), findsOneWidget); expect(find.bySemanticsLabel('workspace-row-1'), findsOneWidget); // ✅ STRONG — proves the actual navigation contract await tester.sendKeyEvent(LogicalKeyboardKey.arrowDown); await tester.pump(); // Verify: focus moved to next row expect(focusedRow, equals(1)); // Verify: navigation fired exactly once expect(switchCount, equals(1)); // Verify: previous row is no longer focusable via Tab final prevRowSemantics = ...; // get semantics of row 0 expect(prevRowSemantics.isFocusable, isFalse); ``` ## Anti-pattern #4 — Hard-coded English labels in DOM matching **Problem**: Browser focus monitor code matches DOM nodes by visible text labels instead of stable semantic identifiers. ```mermaid flowchart TD Match([Need to find DOM element]) --> How{Matching strategy} How -->|"❌ FRAGILE"| Text["querySelector('[aria-label=Settings]')
Match by English label"] How -->|"✅ STABLE"| Ident["querySelector('[flt-semantics-identifier=X]')
Match by semantic identifier"] Text --> Breaks[Breaks on:
• locale change
• label text update
• dynamic content] Ident --> Stable[Stable across:
• all locales
• text changes
• content updates] ``` ### Rule: Always use `flt-semantics-identifier` for DOM matching ```dart // ❌ WRONG — breaks on locale change final el = document.querySelector('[aria-label="Settings"]'); final els = document.querySelectorAll('[aria-label*="Workspace switcher"]'); // ✅ CORRECT — stable identifiers set via Semantics(identifier: ...) final el = document.querySelector('[flt-semantics-identifier="settings-button"]'); final els = document.querySelectorAll('[flt-semantics-identifier^="workspace-row-"]'); ``` ## Anti-pattern #5 — Roving tabindex not fully implemented **Problem**: Setting `Semantics(focusable: true)` on all list items instead of only the active one, breaking the roving tabindex pattern. ```mermaid flowchart TD Pattern([Roving tabindex pattern]) --> Active{Is this the
active/selected item?} Active -->|Yes| Tab0["Semantics(focusable: true)
→ tabindex='0' in DOM
+ focused: true, selected: true"] Active -->|No| TabN1["Semantics(focusable: false)
→ tabindex='-1' in DOM
+ focused: false, selected: false"] Tab0 --> FocusNode["FocusNode(skipTraversal: false)"] TabN1 --> SkipNode["FocusNode(skipTraversal: true)"] FocusNode --> Sync[Call syncBrowserTabIndices()
after state change] SkipNode --> Sync ``` ### Rule: Conditional focusable based on active state + DOM sync ```dart // In _WorkspaceSwitcherRowState: Semantics( identifier: '${workspaceRowIdentifierPrefix}${widget.workspace.name}', focusable: widget.isActive, // ← CONDITIONAL focused: widget.isActive, selected: widget.isActive, child: ... ) // Focus node: FocusNode(skipTraversal: !widget.isActive) // After every selection change: syncBrowserWorkspaceSwitcherRowTabIndices(); // DOM may lag behind Dart state ``` ## Anti-pattern #6 — Browser focus workaround instead of root fix **Problem**: Intercepting `Tab` key to manually move focus instead of fixing the actual focus export order. ```mermaid flowchart TD Approach([Tab order wrong in browser]) --> Options{Fix approach} Options -->|"❌ WORKAROUND"| Intercept["Intercept Tab key in JS
Manually call element.focus()
on the 'correct' target"] Options -->|"✅ ROOT FIX"| FixExport["Fix Semantics sortKey/order
Fix FocusTraversalOrder
Verify DOM tabindex reflects state"] Intercept --> Problems["• Fragile: breaks if elements change
• Partial: doesn't fix Shift+Tab
• Browser-specific: no widget test coverage"] FixExport --> Robust["• Works on all platforms
• Testable in widget tests
• Browser inherits correct order"] ``` ### Rule: Fix the source (Dart Semantics/Focus), not the symptom (DOM focus) Only use browser-side focus manipulation (`element.focus()`, tabindex sync) as a **supplement** when Flutter web engine doesn't correctly export Dart state to DOM. Never as the primary fix. ## Checklist before submitting a keyboard/focus PR ```mermaid flowchart TD A([Ready to submit?]) --> B{All previously-focusable
elements still reachable?} B -->|No| FAIL1[❌ Restore removed focus targets] B -->|Yes| C{Single handler
per key per context?} C -->|No| FAIL2[❌ Remove duplicate handler] C -->|Yes| D{DOM identifiers used
instead of text labels?} D -->|No| FAIL3[❌ Use flt-semantics-identifier] D -->|Yes| E{Widget test exercises
real interaction path?} E -->|No| FAIL4[❌ Add interaction test] E -->|Yes| F{Roving tabindex:
only active item focusable?} F -->|N/A or Yes| G{Browser test confirms
DOM matches Dart state?} F -->|No| FAIL5[❌ Set focusable conditionally] G -->|Not checked| FAIL6[❌ Verify with Playwright or local build] G -->|Yes| OK([✅ Submit PR]) ``` ## Flutter web focus layers — reference ```mermaid graph TB subgraph Dart["Dart Layer (testable with flutter test)"] FTP[FocusTraversalPolicy
OrderedTraversalPolicy] FTO[FocusTraversalOrder
NumericFocusOrder] FN2[FocusNode
skipTraversal] CS2[CallbackShortcuts
KeyboardListener] SEM[Semantics
focusable/focused/selected/identifier/sortKey] end subgraph Engine["Flutter Web Engine (not directly testable)"] SemTree[Semantics tree → DOM] TabCalc[tabindex calculation] FocusBridge[Focus event bridge] end subgraph DOM["Browser DOM (testable with Playwright)"] FLT[flt-semantics nodes] TABIDX[tabindex attributes] ARIA[aria-* attributes] DOMF[document.activeElement] end FTP --> SEM FTO --> SEM FN2 --> SEM SEM --> SemTree SemTree --> FLT TabCalc --> TABIDX FocusBridge --> DOMF style Dart fill:#e8f4fd,stroke:#1a73e8 style Engine fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#e65100 style DOM fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32 ``` **Key gap**: The Engine layer sometimes doesn't update `tabindex` attributes after Dart `Semantics` changes. This is why `syncBrowserWorkspaceSwitcherRowTabIndices()` exists — it manually corrects DOM state when the engine lags. ## Summary of rules | # | Rule | Triggered by | |---|------|-------------| | 1 | One key handler per context — never duplicate browser + Flutter handlers | PR #815 double ArrowDown | | 2 | Never remove focus from visible interactive elements to fix order of others | PR #818 ExcludeFocus on theme toggle | | 3 | Test the user interaction contract, not just semantics metadata | PR #815, #826 weak tests | | 4 | Use `flt-semantics-identifier`, never English text labels, for DOM matching | PR #826 hard-coded labels | | 5 | Roving tabindex: `focusable` must be conditional on active state | PR #827 (our fix for TS-870) | | 6 | Fix focus order at the Dart Semantics source, not via Tab interception | PR #826 Tab interceptor | | 7 | After any Semantics state change, call DOM sync for web platform | Engine lag gap | | 8 | Widget test + Playwright test = complete coverage for keyboard bugs | Widget alone is insufficient | ", 8. "# CodeGraph — code intelligence tools CodeGraph builds a semantic knowledge graph of the codebase and makes it available as CLI commands. The index is pre-built during CI setup, so all commands work immediately without any initialization step. ## When to use CodeGraph vs other tools | Situation | Use | |-----------|-----| | Understand how a feature/flow works end-to-end | `codegraph context ""` | | Find where a class/function/method is defined | `codegraph query ""` | | Trace what a method calls (downstream) | `codegraph callees ""` | | Find who calls a method (upstream) | `codegraph callers ""` | | Check what breaks if you change something | `codegraph impact ""` | | Read a single symbol's source | `codegraph node ""` | | Browse project file structure | `codegraph files` | ## Mandatory usage rule For any task that requires understanding, modifying, or reviewing source code, your first code-navigation command MUST be a CodeGraph command before using `grep`, `find`, `cat`, `sed`, or opening files directly. Use `codegraph context ""` by default. Use `codegraph query ""` only when you already know the symbol name. After editing code, run `codegraph sync` before any further CodeGraph query. Only skip CodeGraph when the task does not involve source-code navigation (for example, a pure Jira/status update or a known single-file text edit). If you skip it, state the reason in the final response. **Prefer CodeGraph over `grep` or `cat` for code understanding** — it uses the pre-built semantic index and returns only relevant, structured results. ## Key commands ### Explore a task or feature ```bash codegraph context "how does the JavaScript runner expose MCP tools" codegraph context "trace the test case generation flow from JQL to Jira ticket creation" ``` Returns: entry-point symbols, related symbols, inline source bodies. ### Find a symbol ```bash codegraph query "JavaScriptExecutor" codegraph query "executeJavaScript" ``` ### Walk call graph ```bash codegraph callees "JobJavaScriptBridge.executeJavaScript" # what it calls codegraph callers "exposeMCPToolsUsingGenerated" # who calls it codegraph impact "MCPToolExecutor.execute" # what changes affect ``` ### Read a symbol ```bash codegraph node "JavaScriptExecutor" codegraph node "JobJavaScriptBridge.initializeJavaScriptContext" ``` ### Check affected tests before a change ```bash codegraph affected src/main/java/com/example/MyService.java ``` ## Sync the index after making code changes If you edit source files during your session, sync the index before querying: ```bash codegraph sync ``` ## Tips - Symbol format: `ClassName` or `ClassName.methodName` - `codegraph context` is the most powerful single command — start there for any "how does X work" question - Results are Markdown-formatted and safe to include directly in your reasoning - The index covers all Java, JavaScript, TypeScript, and Python files in the project " 9. ], 10. If the PR is approved and required checks are still running, do not post another "Rework Complete" comment; the correct state is to wait for CI or merge automation. ## What You Must NOT Do - Do not introduce new logic unrelated to fixing review comments - Do not create new branches or push code — this is automated - Do not change the ticket scope — you are fixing, not re-implementing - Do not ignore any BLOCKING or IMPORTANT review comment ## Tests After all fixes: - Run the full test suite: compile and run all unit tests - If any tests break due to your changes, fix them - If the reviewer asked for new tests, add them ## Output — Two files required ### 1. `outputs/response.md` — Human-readable fix summary (posted as general PR comment) Write in clear SCM-compatible Markdown: ```markdown ## Rework Summary ### Fixed Issues **Thread 1 — `path/to/file.js` line 42** - Issue: - Fix: - Files: `src/foo.js`, `src/bar.js` ### Skipped (with justification) [Only if any suggestions were intentionally skipped] ### Test Status [Pass/Fail + test changes made] ``` ### 2. `outputs/review_replies.json` — Per-thread replies (posted as replies in each discussion thread) For **every thread** in `pr_discussions_raw.json`, produce a reply entry. Use the `rootCommentId` as `inReplyToId` and `id` as `threadId`: ```json { "replies": [ { "inReplyToId": 1234567890, "threadId": "PRRT_kwDOABC123", "reply": "Fixed: extracted validation into `InputValidator` class. See `src/validators/InputValidator.js`." } ] } ``` - `inReplyToId` — from `pr_discussions_raw.json` → `threads[i].rootCommentId` - `threadId` — from `pr_discussions_raw.json` → `threads[i].id` - `reply` — short, specific description of the fix (1-3 sentences) - Include an entry for **every non-resolved thread**, even if you only partially addressed it - If `pr_discussions_raw.json` is absent or empty, write `{ "replies": [] }` **IMPORTANT**: You are only responsible for code fixes and writing these two files — git commit, push, and posting to GitHub are automated. ", "# TrackState Flutter Development Rules Injected via `.dmtools/config.js → additionalInstructions`. The shared `agents/` submodule stays project-independent. ## Stack ```mermaid graph LR App["Flutter / Dart\nlib/"] --> CLI["Dart CLI\nbin/trackstate.dart\n→ dart run trackstate"] App --> Tests["flutter test\nunit + widget"] App --> Config[".dmtools/config.js"] Testing["Automation\ntesting/"] -. owned by test agents .-> App ``` ## Implementation flow ```mermaid flowchart TD Start([Start ticket]) --> Read[Read lib/ pubspec.yaml lib/main.dart\nexisting state-management] Read --> Scope{Touches\npermission/storage\nlayers?} Scope -->|Yes| Permission[See Permission & Storage rules below] Scope -->|No| Impl[Implement following existing patterns] Impl --> Widget{UI changes?} Widget -->|Yes| Keys[Add ValueKey + Semantics label\nUse theme tokens, not hardcoded colors\nLocalize all Text strings] Widget -->|No| Analyze Keys --> Analyze[flutter analyze → 0 issues] Permission --> Analyze Analyze --> Test[flutter test → all pass] Test --> NoTesting{Touched testing/ ?} NoTesting -->|Yes| Justify[Add justification in outputs/response.md] NoTesting -->|No| Output[Write outputs/response.md] Justify --> Output ``` ## Permission & Storage implementation rules These patterns caused the most BLOCKING review cycles. **Read before touching provider/repository/permission code.** ### 1 — Wire capabilities end-to-end through the provider Never hard-code a capability flag to `false` in `_permissionFromRepoJson()` or similar. Every new feature capability (e.g. `supportsReleaseAttachmentWrites`) must be: - Detected in the provider from actual API data - Exposed in the permission/session model - Checked in the repository gate before performing the operation ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant VM as ViewModel participant Repo as Repository participant Provider as Provider participant API as GitHub API VM->>Repo: canUploadAttachment? Repo->>Provider: permissionFromRepo() Provider->>API: repo metadata API-->>Provider: has release write? Provider-->>Repo: supportsReleaseAttachmentWrites=true Repo-->>VM: allowed ✓ ``` ### 2 — Storage-aware permission gates When multiple storage backends exist (local-git, GitHub Releases, etc.), each gate must check the **configured** storage mode, not a generic permission: ``` // ❌ WRONG — generic permission, ignores storage mode if (!permission.canManageAttachments) throw ... // ✅ CORRECT — storage-mode-aware gate if (storageMode == AttachmentStorageMode.githubReleases) { if (!permission.supportsReleaseAttachmentWrites) throw ... } else { if (!permission.canManageAttachments) throw ... } ``` ### 3 — Atomic write ordering (upload → metadata) When implementing two-step writes (binary + JSON manifest): ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant R as Repository participant P as Provider R->>P: uploadBinary() P-->>R: ok R->>P: updateManifest(attachments.json) alt manifest write fails R->>P: rollbackBinary() P-->>R: binary deleted R-->>Caller: throw else success R-->>Caller: updatedAttachment (with POST-write revisionOrOid) end ``` **Never** return a stale `revisionOrOid` computed before the write. Always re-read or refresh the revision after `applyFileChanges`. ### 4 — Download via authenticated asset API, not browser_download_url For private/hosted GitHub repos, `browser_download_url` requires browser redirect and fails for programmatic access. Use: ``` GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/releases/assets/{asset_id} ``` with `Accept: application/octet-stream` and authenticated token. ### 5 — UI state must respect hostedRepositoryAccessMode Callout tone/messaging must branch on `hostedRepositoryAccessMode`: - `writable` → supported/success state - `readOnly` | `disconnected` → restricted/warning state Never collapse `readOnly` and `disconnected` into the same "supported" branch. ### 6 — In-memory validation scaffolding must not persist to disk Synthetic or fallback fields injected for validation (e.g. reserved built-in fields) must be stripped before `saveProjectSettings()` serializes to `config/fields.json`. Keep fallback logic in validation; do not let it reach the persistence layer. ### 7 — Cover all entry points, not just the first one found When fixing a rendering/display bug (e.g. locale label fallback), check **all** surfaces where the same data is displayed: - Search results list - Board column headers - Issue detail view - Settings editor Add a test for each surface you fix. ### 8 — Web (kIsWeb) capability gating for browser-incompatible paths Any code path that uses `Process.run`, `dart:io` File/Directory, or CORS-incompatible hosts **must** be gated on `!kIsWeb` or have a web-specific fallback: ```mermaid flowchart TD Impl([Implementing feature]) --> Check{Uses Process.run,\ndart:io File,\nor non-CORS host?} Check -->|Yes| Web{Has web fallback?} Web -->|No| MUST["MUST add:\n1. kIsWeb gate\n2. Web-specific alternative\n (browser File API, etc.)\n3. Graceful error if impossible"] Web -->|Yes| OK([✅ Both paths covered]) Check -->|No| OK ``` Common web-incompatible patterns: - `Process.run(...)` → throws `Unsupported operation` on web - `File(path).readAsString()` → dart:io unavailable on web - `LocalTrackStateRepository` → uses filesystem, needs `openBrowserLocalRepository` on web - `uploads.github.com` → no CORS headers, needs `!kIsWeb` gate ```dart // ❌ WRONG — crashes on web final result = await Process.run('git', ['status']); // ✅ CORRECT — web-aware with fallback if (kIsWeb) { return _browserLocalFallback(); } else { final result = await Process.run('git', ['status']); } ``` - Import: `import 'package:flutter/foundation.dart' show kIsWeb;` - **Never** let a web execution path reach `Process.run` or `dart:io` — it crashes, not just fails silently. - When adding workspace retry/restore logic, ALWAYS check if the existing `openBrowserLocalRepository` path handles the case. ### 9 — Async startup: always notify listeners after deferred operations When deferring a probe/restore to avoid blocking startup: ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Load as load() participant Deferred as deferredRestore() participant UI as Listeners/UI Load->>Deferred: unawaited(restore()) Load->>UI: notifyListeners() → shell_ready Note over Deferred: restore completes later... Deferred->>UI: ❌ WRONG if no notifyListeners() Deferred->>UI: ✅ MUST notifyListeners() when done ``` **Rules:** - Every `unawaited()` async operation that changes state MUST call `notifyListeners()` on completion - Early-return branches in deferred callbacks MUST still notify if the state is meaningful (e.g. `githubAuthorizationCodeReturned`) - Test both: (a) startup completes without blocking, AND (b) deferred result eventually surfaces ### 10 — Workspace state: test ALL state transitions, not just happy path When implementing workspace state changes (retry, restore, switch): ```mermaid flowchart TD Change([State change logic]) --> Paths{All paths\ncovered?} Paths -->|"Only success path"| BAD["❌ Missing:\n- What if reopen fails on web?\n- What if directory doesn't match?\n- What if token expired?"] Paths -->|"Success + all error branches"| GOOD["✅ Each branch either:\n1. Transitions to correct state\n2. Shows error UI\n3. Notifies listeners"] ``` **Rules:** - Never reuse `previousViewModel.repository` for a "restored" workspace — create fresh state - If `_prepareWorkspaceSwitch()` can fail on web, add `try/catch` with browser-specific fallback - Regression test must verify the VISIBLE repository changed, not just workspace metadata | # | Rule | |---|------| | 1 | Read `lib/`, `pubspec.yaml`, `lib/main.dart`, existing patterns before writing code | | 2 | Add packages via `flutter pub add`, never hand-edit `pubspec.yaml` | | 3 | `ValueKey` (kebab-case) on every user-facing or automation-targeted widget | | 4 | `Semantics(label:...)` on every `IconButton`, `GestureDetector`, custom interactive widget | | 5 | Theme tokens only — no hardcoded `Color(0xFF...)` or pixel sizes | | 6 | All `Text()` content must go through the localization system | | 7 | For CLI: validate `--path`, run from repo root, keep JSON response schema stable | | 8 | Do not touch `testing/` unless ticket requires it; justify in `outputs/response.md` | | 9 | `flutter analyze` → 0 issues; `flutter test` → all pass before finishing | | 10 | Null safety: no `dynamic`, no unjustified `!` | | 11 | Every code path that uses `Process.run` or `dart:io` MUST have a `kIsWeb` gate or web fallback | | 12 | Every `unawaited()` deferred operation that changes state MUST call `notifyListeners()` on completion | | 13 | Workspace state changes must cover ALL branches (success, web failure, directory mismatch, token expired) | ## Bug-fix additional rules - Ticket returned to dev → read prior Jira comments + previous PR diffs before changing anything - CLI bug → test both happy path and exact error path from ticket - Check `git log --oneline lib/ | head -20` before fixing to see recent changes to the same files - **Web platform**: before fixing any workspace/startup bug, check if the failing path uses `Process.run` or native file access → add web fallback via `openBrowserLocalRepository` - **Startup bugs**: check whether the fix blocks `shell_ready` — users must see the interactive shell quickly even if background probes are still running - **State bugs**: verify the fix handles the case where the previous workspace was hosted but the target is local (never reuse `previousViewModel.repository`) - **Scope**: fix ONLY the bug described in the ticket. Do not add unrelated JQL/locale/settings changes — they will be BLOCKING rejected ## Output (`outputs/response.md`) Must include: issues/notes · approach · files modified · test coverage · `flutter analyze` + `flutter test` result ", "# Flutter Web Focus & Keyboard Navigation Rules Injected via `.dmtools/config.js → additionalInstructions`. Addresses recurring BLOCKING review issues in keyboard/focus/accessibility PRs. ## Architecture — Flutter focus vs Browser DOM focus ```mermaid graph TD subgraph Flutter["Flutter Engine (Dart)"] FT[FocusTraversalPolicy] FS[FocusScopeNode] FN[FocusNode] CS[CallbackShortcuts] Sem[Semantics widget] end subgraph Browser["Browser DOM (Web only)"] TI[tabindex attribute] FLTD["flt-semantics DOM nodes"] KE[KeyboardEvent] end Sem -->|"exports"| FLTD FN -->|"maps to"| TI CS -->|"handles"| KE style Flutter fill:#e8f4fd,stroke:#1a73e8 style Browser fill:#fef7e0,stroke:#f9ab00 ``` **Critical insight**: Flutter widget tests exercise the `Flutter Engine` layer only. The `Browser DOM` layer can diverge. A widget test passing does NOT prove the browser keyboard path works. ## Decision tree — Fixing a keyboard/focus bug ```mermaid flowchart TD Start([Bug: keyboard navigation broken]) --> Where{Where does it fail?} Where -->|Widget test| WidgetFix[Fix FocusNode/Semantics/Shortcuts in Dart] Where -->|Browser only| BrowserAnalyze{Is the Dart Semantics
export correct?} BrowserAnalyze -->|No| FixSemantics[Fix Semantics widget props
focusable/selected/sortKey] BrowserAnalyze -->|Yes| DOMGap[Flutter web engine gap:
DOM doesn't reflect Dart state] DOMGap --> BridgeNeeded{Can we fix via
DOM bridge?} BridgeNeeded -->|Yes| DOMBridge[Add browser_*_focus_monitor_web.dart
sync function] BridgeNeeded -->|No| Escalate[Document gap in bug_description.md
→ needs Flutter engine fix] WidgetFix --> Verify FixSemantics --> Verify DOMBridge --> Verify Verify{Verify fix} Verify --> WidgetTest[✅ Widget test for Dart layer] Verify --> BrowserTest[✅ Playwright test for DOM layer] WidgetTest --> Both[Both must pass] BrowserTest --> Both ``` ## Anti-pattern #1 — Duplicate event handlers (racing) **Problem**: Adding a browser-level `window.onKeyDown` handler for a key that Flutter already handles via `CallbackShortcuts` or `KeyboardListener`. ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant User as User presses ArrowDown participant DOM as Browser DOM participant Bridge as browser_focus_monitor_web.dart participant Flutter as Flutter CallbackShortcuts User->>DOM: keydown: ArrowDown DOM->>Bridge: window.onKeyDown fires Bridge->>Flutter: _switchToAdjacentWorkspace(+1) DOM->>Flutter: Flutter engine receives same key Flutter->>Flutter: CallbackShortcuts fires Flutter->>Flutter: _switchToAdjacentWorkspace(+1) ← AGAIN! Note over Flutter: ❌ Double navigation! ``` ### Rule: One handler per key per context ```mermaid flowchart TD Key([Key needs handling]) --> FocusOwner{Who owns focus
when key fires?} FocusOwner -->|Flutter FocusNode| UseDart[Handle in CallbackShortcuts
or KeyboardListener in Dart] FocusOwner -->|Browser DOM node| UseBridge[Handle in browser_*_monitor_web.dart
via window.onKeyDown] FocusOwner -->|Both possible| Guard[Add mutual exclusion guard:
check actual focus owner before dispatch] UseDart --> NoWeb[❌ Do NOT add duplicate window.onKeyDown] UseBridge --> NoDart[❌ Do NOT add duplicate CallbackShortcuts] ``` **Implementation pattern:** ```dart // ✅ CORRECT — single handler with context check void _handleBrowserKeyDown(html.KeyboardEvent event) { if (!isBrowserFocusOnWorkspaceSwitcherRow()) return; // guard if (event.key == 'ArrowDown') { event.preventDefault(); _switchToAdjacentWorkspace(1); } } // ❌ WRONG — browser handler + Flutter CallbackShortcuts both handle ArrowDown // This causes double-fire when focus transitions between layers ``` ## Anti-pattern #2 — Fixing focus order by removing other elements **Problem**: Using `ExcludeFocus`, `skipTraversal: true`, or `canRequestFocus: false` on visible interactive elements to "fix" the order of a different element. ```mermaid flowchart TD Bug([Bug: Element X not in tab order]) --> Wrong{Fix approach} Wrong -->|"❌ WRONG"| Exclude[ExcludeFocus on element Y
to make X appear 'next'] Wrong -->|"✅ CORRECT"| Order[FocusTraversalOrder on X
to place it in correct position] Exclude --> Breaks[Breaks: Element Y now
unreachable by keyboard] Order --> Works[Works: All elements remain
keyboard-accessible] ``` ### Rule: Never remove keyboard access from visible interactive elements Before submitting a focus-order fix, verify: 1. **All previously-focusable elements** in the same area are still keyboard-reachable 2. **The fix adds/reorders**, never removes, focus targets 3. **Check adjacent controls**: theme toggle, sync pill, CTAs, search — all must stay in tab order ```dart // ❌ WRONG — hides theme toggle from keyboard to "fix" switcher position ExcludeFocus(child: themeToggleButton) // ✅ CORRECT — explicit ordering keeps all elements reachable FocusTraversalOrder( order: NumericFocusOrder(3.0), child: themeToggleButton, ) ``` ## Anti-pattern #3 — Widget test covers metadata, not interaction **Problem**: Test checks that semantics identifiers/sort keys exist, but doesn't exercise the actual user interaction that fails. ```mermaid flowchart TD Test([Writing regression test]) --> What{What to assert?} What -->|"❌ Weak"| Meta[Assert identifier exists
Assert sort key value
Assert node count] What -->|"✅ Strong"| Interaction[Simulate full interaction:
open → navigate → verify focus moved
→ verify action fired exactly once] Meta --> FalseGreen[Can pass while bug persists
in real user flow] Interaction --> TrueSignal[Fails only when real
user path is broken] ``` ### Rule: Test the user contract, not the implementation metadata ```dart // ❌ WEAK — proves identifiers exist, not that interaction works expect(find.bySemanticsLabel('workspace-row-0'), findsOneWidget); expect(find.bySemanticsLabel('workspace-row-1'), findsOneWidget); // ✅ STRONG — proves the actual navigation contract await tester.sendKeyEvent(LogicalKeyboardKey.arrowDown); await tester.pump(); // Verify: focus moved to next row expect(focusedRow, equals(1)); // Verify: navigation fired exactly once expect(switchCount, equals(1)); // Verify: previous row is no longer focusable via Tab final prevRowSemantics = ...; // get semantics of row 0 expect(prevRowSemantics.isFocusable, isFalse); ``` ## Anti-pattern #4 — Hard-coded English labels in DOM matching **Problem**: Browser focus monitor code matches DOM nodes by visible text labels instead of stable semantic identifiers. ```mermaid flowchart TD Match([Need to find DOM element]) --> How{Matching strategy} How -->|"❌ FRAGILE"| Text["querySelector('[aria-label=Settings]')
Match by English label"] How -->|"✅ STABLE"| Ident["querySelector('[flt-semantics-identifier=X]')
Match by semantic identifier"] Text --> Breaks[Breaks on:
• locale change
• label text update
• dynamic content] Ident --> Stable[Stable across:
• all locales
• text changes
• content updates] ``` ### Rule: Always use `flt-semantics-identifier` for DOM matching ```dart // ❌ WRONG — breaks on locale change final el = document.querySelector('[aria-label="Settings"]'); final els = document.querySelectorAll('[aria-label*="Workspace switcher"]'); // ✅ CORRECT — stable identifiers set via Semantics(identifier: ...) final el = document.querySelector('[flt-semantics-identifier="settings-button"]'); final els = document.querySelectorAll('[flt-semantics-identifier^="workspace-row-"]'); ``` ## Anti-pattern #5 — Roving tabindex not fully implemented **Problem**: Setting `Semantics(focusable: true)` on all list items instead of only the active one, breaking the roving tabindex pattern. ```mermaid flowchart TD Pattern([Roving tabindex pattern]) --> Active{Is this the
active/selected item?} Active -->|Yes| Tab0["Semantics(focusable: true)
→ tabindex='0' in DOM
+ focused: true, selected: true"] Active -->|No| TabN1["Semantics(focusable: false)
→ tabindex='-1' in DOM
+ focused: false, selected: false"] Tab0 --> FocusNode["FocusNode(skipTraversal: false)"] TabN1 --> SkipNode["FocusNode(skipTraversal: true)"] FocusNode --> Sync[Call syncBrowserTabIndices()
after state change] SkipNode --> Sync ``` ### Rule: Conditional focusable based on active state + DOM sync ```dart // In _WorkspaceSwitcherRowState: Semantics( identifier: '${workspaceRowIdentifierPrefix}${widget.workspace.name}', focusable: widget.isActive, // ← CONDITIONAL focused: widget.isActive, selected: widget.isActive, child: ... ) // Focus node: FocusNode(skipTraversal: !widget.isActive) // After every selection change: syncBrowserWorkspaceSwitcherRowTabIndices(); // DOM may lag behind Dart state ``` ## Anti-pattern #6 — Browser focus workaround instead of root fix **Problem**: Intercepting `Tab` key to manually move focus instead of fixing the actual focus export order. ```mermaid flowchart TD Approach([Tab order wrong in browser]) --> Options{Fix approach} Options -->|"❌ WORKAROUND"| Intercept["Intercept Tab key in JS
Manually call element.focus()
on the 'correct' target"] Options -->|"✅ ROOT FIX"| FixExport["Fix Semantics sortKey/order
Fix FocusTraversalOrder
Verify DOM tabindex reflects state"] Intercept --> Problems["• Fragile: breaks if elements change
• Partial: doesn't fix Shift+Tab
• Browser-specific: no widget test coverage"] FixExport --> Robust["• Works on all platforms
• Testable in widget tests
• Browser inherits correct order"] ``` ### Rule: Fix the source (Dart Semantics/Focus), not the symptom (DOM focus) Only use browser-side focus manipulation (`element.focus()`, tabindex sync) as a **supplement** when Flutter web engine doesn't correctly export Dart state to DOM. Never as the primary fix. ## Checklist before submitting a keyboard/focus PR ```mermaid flowchart TD A([Ready to submit?]) --> B{All previously-focusable
elements still reachable?} B -->|No| FAIL1[❌ Restore removed focus targets] B -->|Yes| C{Single handler
per key per context?} C -->|No| FAIL2[❌ Remove duplicate handler] C -->|Yes| D{DOM identifiers used
instead of text labels?} D -->|No| FAIL3[❌ Use flt-semantics-identifier] D -->|Yes| E{Widget test exercises
real interaction path?} E -->|No| FAIL4[❌ Add interaction test] E -->|Yes| F{Roving tabindex:
only active item focusable?} F -->|N/A or Yes| G{Browser test confirms
DOM matches Dart state?} F -->|No| FAIL5[❌ Set focusable conditionally] G -->|Not checked| FAIL6[❌ Verify with Playwright or local build] G -->|Yes| OK([✅ Submit PR]) ``` ## Flutter web focus layers — reference ```mermaid graph TB subgraph Dart["Dart Layer (testable with flutter test)"] FTP[FocusTraversalPolicy
OrderedTraversalPolicy] FTO[FocusTraversalOrder
NumericFocusOrder] FN2[FocusNode
skipTraversal] CS2[CallbackShortcuts
KeyboardListener] SEM[Semantics
focusable/focused/selected/identifier/sortKey] end subgraph Engine["Flutter Web Engine (not directly testable)"] SemTree[Semantics tree → DOM] TabCalc[tabindex calculation] FocusBridge[Focus event bridge] end subgraph DOM["Browser DOM (testable with Playwright)"] FLT[flt-semantics nodes] TABIDX[tabindex attributes] ARIA[aria-* attributes] DOMF[document.activeElement] end FTP --> SEM FTO --> SEM FN2 --> SEM SEM --> SemTree SemTree --> FLT TabCalc --> TABIDX FocusBridge --> DOMF style Dart fill:#e8f4fd,stroke:#1a73e8 style Engine fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#e65100 style DOM fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32 ``` **Key gap**: The Engine layer sometimes doesn't update `tabindex` attributes after Dart `Semantics` changes. This is why `syncBrowserWorkspaceSwitcherRowTabIndices()` exists — it manually corrects DOM state when the engine lags. ## Summary of rules | # | Rule | Triggered by | |---|------|-------------| | 1 | One key handler per context — never duplicate browser + Flutter handlers | PR #815 double ArrowDown | | 2 | Never remove focus from visible interactive elements to fix order of others | PR #818 ExcludeFocus on theme toggle | | 3 | Test the user interaction contract, not just semantics metadata | PR #815, #826 weak tests | | 4 | Use `flt-semantics-identifier`, never English text labels, for DOM matching | PR #826 hard-coded labels | | 5 | Roving tabindex: `focusable` must be conditional on active state | PR #827 (our fix for TS-870) | | 6 | Fix focus order at the Dart Semantics source, not via Tab interception | PR #826 Tab interceptor | | 7 | After any Semantics state change, call DOM sync for web platform | Engine lag gap | | 8 | Widget test + Playwright test = complete coverage for keyboard bugs | Widget alone is insufficient | ", "# CodeGraph — code intelligence tools CodeGraph builds a semantic knowledge graph of the codebase and makes it available as CLI commands. The index is pre-built during CI setup, so all commands work immediately without any initialization step. ## When to use CodeGraph vs other tools | Situation | Use | |-----------|-----| | Understand how a feature/flow works end-to-end | `codegraph context ""` | | Find where a class/function/method is defined | `codegraph query ""` | | Trace what a method calls (downstream) | `codegraph callees ""` | | Find who calls a method (upstream) | `codegraph callers ""` | | Check what breaks if you change something | `codegraph impact ""` | | Read a single symbol's source | `codegraph node ""` | | Browse project file structure | `codegraph files` | ## Mandatory usage rule For any task that requires understanding, modifying, or reviewing source code, your first code-navigation command MUST be a CodeGraph command before using `grep`, `find`, `cat`, `sed`, or opening files directly. Use `codegraph context ""` by default. Use `codegraph query ""` only when you already know the symbol name. After editing code, run `codegraph sync` before any further CodeGraph query. Only skip CodeGraph when the task does not involve source-code navigation (for example, a pure Jira/status update or a known single-file text edit). If you skip it, state the reason in the final response. **Prefer CodeGraph over `grep` or `cat` for code understanding** — it uses the pre-built semantic index and returns only relevant, structured results. ## Key commands ### Explore a task or feature ```bash codegraph context "how does the JavaScript runner expose MCP tools" codegraph context "trace the test case generation flow from JQL to Jira ticket creation" ``` Returns: entry-point symbols, related symbols, inline source bodies. ### Find a symbol ```bash codegraph query "JavaScriptExecutor" codegraph query "executeJavaScript" ``` ### Walk call graph ```bash codegraph callees "JobJavaScriptBridge.executeJavaScript" # what it calls codegraph callers "exposeMCPToolsUsingGenerated" # who calls it codegraph impact "MCPToolExecutor.execute" # what changes affect ``` ### Read a symbol ```bash codegraph node "JavaScriptExecutor" codegraph node "JobJavaScriptBridge.initializeJavaScriptContext" ``` ### Check affected tests before a change ```bash codegraph affected src/main/java/com/example/MyService.java ``` ## Sync the index after making code changes If you edit source files during your session, sync the index before querying: ```bash codegraph sync ``` ## Tips - Symbol format: `ClassName` or `ClassName.methodName` - `codegraph context` is the most powerful single command — start there for any "how does X work" question - Results are Markdown-formatted and safe to include directly in your reasoning - The index covers all Java, JavaScript, TypeScript, and Python files in the project " ], "fewShots": "", "questions": [], "formattingRules": "outputs/response.md must be a markdown document with sections: ## Issues/Notes (if any), ## Approach, ## Files Modified, ## Test Coverage ", "aiRole": "Senior Developer Engineer focused on code fixes", "tasks": [] }

716.     if (mutator == null) {

718.         switch (change) {
719.           case RepositoryTextFileChange():
720.             await _provider.writeTextFile(
721.               RepositoryWriteRequest(
722.                 path: change.path,

726.                 expectedRevision: change.expectedRevision,
727.               ),
728.             );
729.           case RepositoryDeleteFileChange():
730.             throw const TrackStateRepositoryException(
731.               'This repository implementation does not support deleting locale configuration files.',
732.             );
733.           case RepositoryBinaryFileChange():
734.             throw const TrackStateRepositoryException(
735.               'Project settings do not support binary file changes.',
736.             );
737.         }
738.       }
739.     } else {
740.       final commitResult = await mutator.applyFileChanges(
741.         RepositoryFileChangeRequest(
742.           branch: writeBranch,
743.           message: 'Update project settings',
744.           changes: changes,
745.         ),
746.       );
747.       if (!commitResult.createdCommit) {
748.         throw const TrackStateRepositoryException(
749.           projectSettingsNoCommitProducedMessage,
750.         );
751.       }
752.     }
753.     final refreshedSnapshot = await loadSnapshot();
754.     _snapshot = refreshedSnapshot;
755.     return refreshedSnapshot;
756.   }

667.       RepositoryTextFileChange(
668.         path: _joinPath(configRoot, 'resolutions.json'),
669.         content:
670.             '${jsonEncode(_settingsConfigEntriesJson(persistedSettings.resolutionDefinitions))}\n',
671.         expectedRevision: await _existingRevision(
672.           path: _joinPath(configRoot, 'resolutions.json'),
673.           ref: writeBranch,
674.           blobPaths: blobPaths,
675.           blobRevisions: blobRevisions,
676.         ),
677.       ),
678.     ];
679.     for (final locale in normalizedSettings.effectiveSupportedLocales) {
680.       final path = _joinPath(configRoot, 'i18n/$locale.json');
681.       changes.add(
682.         RepositoryTextFileChange(
683.           path: path,
684.           content:
685.               '${jsonEncode(_localizedLabelsJson(persistedSettings, locale))}\n',
686.           expectedRevision: await _existingRevision(
687.             path: path,
688.             ref: writeBranch,
689.             blobPaths: blobPaths,
690.             blobRevisions: blobRevisions,
691.           ),
692.         ),
693.       );
694.     }
695.     for (final locale in existingSupportedLocales) {
696.       if (normalizedSettings.effectiveSupportedLocales.contains(locale)) {
697.         continue;
698.       }
699.       final path = _joinPath(configRoot, 'i18n/$locale.json');
700.       if (!blobPaths.contains(path)) {
701.         continue;
702.       }
703.       changes.add(
704.         RepositoryDeleteFileChange(
705.           path: path,
706.           expectedRevision: await _existingRevision(
707.             path: path,
708.             ref: writeBranch,
709.             blobPaths: blobPaths,
710.             blobRevisions: blobRevisions,
711.           ),
712.         ),
713.       );
714.     }
715.     final mutator = _provider as RepositoryFileMutator?;
716.     if (mutator == null) {
717.       for (final change in changes) {
718.         switch (change) {
719.           case RepositoryTextFileChange():
720.             await _provider.writeTextFile(
721.               RepositoryWriteRequest(
722.                 path: change.path,
723.                 content: change.content,
724.                 message: 'Update project settings',
725.                 branch: writeBranch,
726.                 expectedRevision: change.expectedRevision,
727.               ),
728.             );
729.           case RepositoryDeleteFileChange():
730.             throw const TrackStateRepositoryException(
731.               'This repository implementation does not support deleting locale configuration files.',
732.             );
733.           case RepositoryBinaryFileChange():
734.             throw const TrackStateRepositoryException(
735.               'Project settings do not support binary file changes.',
736.             );
737.         }
738.       }
739.     } else {
740.       final commitResult = await mutator.applyFileChanges(
741.         RepositoryFileChangeRequest(
742.           branch: writeBranch,
743.           message: 'Update project settings',
744.           changes: changes,
745.         ),
746.       );
747.       if (!commitResult.createdCommit) {
748.         throw const TrackStateRepositoryException(
749.           projectSettingsNoCommitProducedMessage,
750.         );
751.       }
752.     }
753.     final refreshedSnapshot = await loadSnapshot();
754.     _snapshot = refreshedSnapshot;
755.     return refreshedSnapshot;
756.   }

633.     List<String> arguments,

635.     final parser = ArgParser(allowTrailingOptions: false)
636.       ..addFlag('help', abbr: 'h', negatable: false)

641.       )

643.       ..addOption(
644.         'path',
645.         help:
646.             'Local repository path. Defaults to the current working directory.',
647.       )
648.       ..addOption('branch', help: 'Branch to use for the attachment command.')
649.       ..addOption('token', help: 'Hosted access token.')
650.       ..addOption('issue', help: 'Issue key that will receive the attachment.')
651.       ..addOption('file', help: 'Source file to upload.')
652.       ..addOption(
653.         'name',
654.         help: 'Optional stored attachment name. Defaults to the file name.',
655.       )
656.       ..addOption(
657.         'output',
658.         defaultsTo: 'json',
659.         allowed: TrackStateCliOutput.values.map((value) => value.name).toList(),
660.         help: 'Output format. Defaults to json.',
661.       );
662. 
663.     late final ArgResults results;
664.     try {
665.       results = parser.parse(arguments);
666.     } on FormatException catch (error) {
667.       throw _TrackStateCliException(

672.         details: <String, Object?>{'arguments': arguments},
673.       );
674.     }
675.

677.       return TrackStateCliExecution.success(

680.       );
681.     }

683.     _validateSingleOptionOccurrence(

687.       message:
688.           'Only one file may be provided per invocation; duplicate "--file" options are not allowed.',
689.     );
690. 
691.     final output = TrackStateCliOutput.values.byName(
692.       results['output']!.toString(),
693.     );
694.     final target = await _resolveTarget(results);
695.     final issueKey = results['issue']?.toString().trim() ?? '';
696.     if (issueKey.isEmpty) {
697.       throw _TrackStateCliException(

700.         message: 'Missing required option "--issue".',
701.         exitCode: 2,
702.         details: const <String, Object?>{'option': 'issue'},
703.       );
704.     }
705. 
706.     final configuredFilePath = results['file']?.toString().trim() ?? '';
707.     if (configuredFilePath.isEmpty) {
708.       throw _TrackStateCliException(
709.         code: 'INVALID_ATTACHMENT',
710.         category: TrackStateCliErrorCategory.validation,
711.         message: 'Missing required option "--file".',
712.         exitCode: 2,
713.         details: const <String, Object?>{'option': 'file'},
714.       );
715.     }
716.     final resolvedFilePath = _environment.resolvePath(configuredFilePath);
717.     final sourceFile = File(resolvedFilePath);
718.     if (!await sourceFile.exists()) {
719.       throw _TrackStateCliException(
720.         code: 'INVALID_ATTACHMENT',
721.         category: TrackStateCliErrorCategory.validation,
722.         message: 'Attachment source file was not found: $resolvedFilePath',
723.         exitCode: 2,
724.         details: <String, Object?>{'file': resolvedFilePath},
725.       );
726.     }
727.     final bytes = await sourceFile.readAsBytes();
728.     final sourceName = _fileNameFromPath(resolvedFilePath);
729.     final attachmentName =
730.         results['name']?.toString().trim().ifEmpty(sourceName) ?? sourceName;
731. 
732.     try {
733.       return await switch (target.type) {
734.         TrackStateCliTargetType.local => _runLocalAttachmentUpload(
735.           target,
736.           output,

744.           output,
745.           issueKey: issueKey,

747.           sourceName: sourceName,

252.     test(
253.       'includes projectConfig statuses and workflows in session output',
254.       () async {
255.         final snapshot = TrackerSnapshot(
256.           project: const ProjectConfig(
257.             key: 'DEMO',
258.             name: 'Demo',
259.             repository: 'owner/demo',
260.             branch: 'main',
261.             defaultLocale: 'en',
262.             issueTypeDefinitions: <TrackStateConfigEntry>[
263.               TrackStateConfigEntry(
264.                 id: 'story',
265.                 name: 'Story',
266.                 workflowId: 'delivery',
267.               ),
268.             ],
269.             statusDefinitions: <TrackStateConfigEntry>[
270.               TrackStateConfigEntry(id: 'todo', name: 'To Do', category: 'new'),
271.               TrackStateConfigEntry(
272.                 id: 'in-review',
273.                 name: 'In Review',
274.                 category: 'indeterminate',
275.               ),
276.               TrackStateConfigEntry(id: 'done', name: 'Done', category: 'done'),
277.             ],
278.             fieldDefinitions: <TrackStateFieldDefinition>[
279.               TrackStateFieldDefinition(
280.                 id: 'summary',
281.                 name: 'Summary',
282.                 type: 'string',
283.                 required: true,
284.               ),
285.             ],
286.             workflowDefinitions: <TrackStateWorkflowDefinition>[
287.               TrackStateWorkflowDefinition(
288.                 id: 'delivery',
289.                 name: 'Delivery',
290.                 statusIds: <String>['todo', 'in-review', 'done'],
291.                 transitions: <TrackStateWorkflowTransition>[
292.                   TrackStateWorkflowTransition(
293.                     id: 'review',
294.                     name: 'Request review',
295.                     fromStatusId: 'todo',
296.                     toStatusId: 'in-review',
297.                   ),
298.                   TrackStateWorkflowTransition(
299.                     id: 'complete',
300.                     name: 'Complete',
301.                     fromStatusId: 'in-review',
302.                     toStatusId: 'done',
303.                   ),
304.                 ],
305.               ),
306.             ],
307.           ),
308.           issues: const <TrackStateIssue>[],
309.         );
310.         final cli = TrackStateCli(
311.           environment: const TrackStateCliEnvironment(
312.             workingDirectory: '/workspace/repo',
313.           ),
314.           providerFactory: _FakeTrackStateCliProviderFactory(
315.             localProvider: _FakeLocalGitTrackStateProvider(
316.               repositoryPath: '/workspace/repo',
317.               branch: 'main',
318.               user: const RepositoryUser(
319.                 login: 'local@example.com',
320.                 displayName: 'Local User',
321.               ),
322.               permission: const RepositoryPermission(
323.                 canRead: true,
324.                 canWrite: true,
325.                 isAdmin: false,
326.               ),
327.             ),
328.           ),
329.           repositoryFactory: _FakeTrackStateCliRepositoryFactory(
330.             localRepository: _FakeSearchRepository(snapshot: snapshot),
331.           ),
332.         );
333. 
334.         final result = await cli.run(const <String>[
335.           'session',
336.           '--target',
337.           'local',
338.         ]);
339.         final json = jsonDecode(result.stdout) as Map<String, Object?>;
340.         final data = json['data']! as Map<String, Object?>;
341.         final projectConfig = data['projectConfig']! as Map<String, Object?>;
342.         final statuses = projectConfig['statuses']! as List<Object?>;
343.         final workflows = projectConfig['workflows']! as List<Object?>;
344.         final delivery = workflows.single as Map<String, Object?>;
345.         final transitions = delivery['transitions']! as List<Object?>;
346.         final reviewTransition = transitions.first as Map<String, Object?>;
347. 
348.         expect(result.exitCode, 0);
349.         expect(
350.           statuses.any(
351.             (status) =>
352.                 (status as Map<String, Object?>)['id'] == 'in-review' &&
353.                 status['name'] == 'In Review' &&
354.                 status['category'] == 'indeterminate',
355.           ),
356.           isTrue,
357.         );
358.         expect(delivery['id'], 'delivery');
359.         expect(delivery['statuses'], <Map<String, Object?>>[
360.           <String, Object?>{'id': 'todo', 'name': 'To Do'},
361.           <String, Object?>{'id': 'in-review', 'name': 'In Review'},
362.           <String, Object?>{'id': 'done', 'name': 'Done'},
363.         ]);
364.         expect(reviewTransition['name'], 'Request review');
365.         expect(reviewTransition['to'], <String, Object?>{
366.           'id': 'in-review',
367.           'name': 'In Review',
368.         });
369.       },
370.     );
371. 
372.     test('rejects hosted targets without credentials', () async {
373.       final cli = TrackStateCli(
374.         environment: const TrackStateCliEnvironment(
375.           environment: <String, String>{},
376.           readGhAuthToken: _emptyGhToken,
377.         ),
378.       );
379. 
380.       final result = await cli.run(const <String>[

266.                 workflowId: 'delivery',
267.               ),
268.             ],
269.             statusDefinitions: <TrackStateConfigEntry>[
270.               TrackStateConfigEntry(id: 'todo', name: 'To Do', category: 'new'),
271.               TrackStateConfigEntry(
272.                 id: 'in-review',
273.                 name: 'In Review',
274.                 category: 'indeterminate',
275.               ),
276.               TrackStateConfigEntry(id: 'done', name: 'Done', category: 'done'),
277.             ],
278.             fieldDefinitions: <TrackStateFieldDefinition>[
279.               TrackStateFieldDefinition(
280.                 id: 'summary',
281.                 name: 'Summary',
282.                 type: 'string',
283.                 required: true,
284.               ),
285.             ],
286.             workflowDefinitions: <TrackStateWorkflowDefinition>[
287.               TrackStateWorkflowDefinition(
288.                 id: 'delivery',
289.                 name: 'Delivery',
290.                 statusIds: <String>['todo', 'in-review', 'done'],
291.                 transitions: <TrackStateWorkflowTransition>[
292.                   TrackStateWorkflowTransition(
293.                     id: 'review',
294.                     name: 'Request review',
295.                     fromStatusId: 'todo',
296.                     toStatusId: 'in-review',
297.                   ),
298.                   TrackStateWorkflowTransition(
299.                     id: 'complete',
300.                     name: 'Complete',
301.                     fromStatusId: 'in-review',
302.                     toStatusId: 'done',
303.                   ),
304.                 ],
305.               ),
306.             ],
307.           ),
308.           issues: const <TrackStateIssue>[],
309.         );
310.         final cli = TrackStateCli(
311.           environment: const TrackStateCliEnvironment(
312.             workingDirectory: '/workspace/repo',
313.           ),
314.           providerFactory: _FakeTrackStateCliProviderFactory(
315.             localProvider: _FakeLocalGitTrackStateProvider(
316.               repositoryPath: '/workspace/repo',
317.               branch: 'main',
318.               user: const RepositoryUser(
319.                 login: 'local@example.com',
320.                 displayName: 'Local User',
321.               ),
322.               permission: const RepositoryPermission(
323.                 canRead: true,
324.                 canWrite: true,
325.                 isAdmin: false,
326.               ),
327.             ),
328.           ),
329.           repositoryFactory: _FakeTrackStateCliRepositoryFactory(
330.             localRepository: _FakeSearchRepository(snapshot: snapshot),
331.           ),
332.         );
333. 
334.         final result = await cli.run(const <String>[
335.           'session',
336.           '--target',
337.           'local',
338.         ]);
339.         final json = jsonDecode(result.stdout) as Map<String, Object?>;
340.         final data = json['data']! as Map<String, Object?>;
341.         final projectConfig = data['projectConfig']! as Map<String, Object?>;
342.         final statuses = projectConfig['statuses']! as List<Object?>;
343.         final workflows = projectConfig['workflows']! as List<Object?>;
344.         final delivery = workflows.single as Map<String, Object?>;
345.         final transitions = delivery['transitions']! as List<Object?>;
346.         final reviewTransition = transitions.first as Map<String, Object?>;
347. 
348.         expect(result.exitCode, 0);
349.         expect(
350.           statuses.any(
351.             (status) =>
352.                 (status as Map<String, Object?>)['id'] == 'in-review' &&
353.                 status['name'] == 'In Review' &&
354.                 status['category'] == 'indeterminate',
355.           ),
356.           isTrue,
357.         );
358.         expect(delivery['id'], 'delivery');
359.         expect(delivery['statuses'], <Map<String, Object?>>[
360.           <String, Object?>{'id': 'todo', 'name': 'To Do'},
361.           <String, Object?>{'id': 'in-review', 'name': 'In Review'},
362.           <String, Object?>{'id': 'done', 'name': 'Done'},
363.         ]);
364.         expect(reviewTransition['name'], 'Request review');
365.         expect(reviewTransition['to'], <String, Object?>{
366.           'id': 'in-review',
367.           'name': 'In Review',
368.         });
369.       },
370.     );

toolResult.textResultForLlm:
1. name: AI Teammate
2. 
3. run-name: "${{ inputs.config_file }} : ${{ inputs.concurrency_key }}"
4. 
5. on:
6.   workflow_dispatch:
7.     inputs:
8.       config_file:
9.         description: "Path to config"
10.         required: true
11.       encoded_config:
12.         description: "Encoded or JSON Agent Config"
13.         required: false
14.       concurrency_key:
15.         description: "Ticket key for concurrency grouping (e.g., TS-1)"
16.         required: true
17.       input_jql:
18.         description: "Optional Jira input JQL; defaults to key = concurrency_key"
19.         required: false
20.       project_key:
21.         description: "Project key for multi-project config selection"
22.         required: false
23. 
24. permissions:
25.   contents: write
26.   pull-requests: write
27.   actions: read
28. 
29. jobs:
30.   ai-teammate:
31.     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
32.     concurrency:
33.       group: ai-teammate-${{ inputs.config_file }}-${{ inputs.concurrency_key }}
34.       cancel-in-progress: false
35. 
36.     steps:
37.       - name: Job info
38.         run: |
39.           echo "Config file: ${{ inputs.config_file }}"
40.           echo "Concurrency key: ${{ inputs.concurrency_key }}"
41.           echo "Display key: ${{ inputs.display_key || inputs.concurrency_key }}"
42.           if [ -n "${{ inputs.encoded_config }}" ]; then
43.             echo "Encoded config: provided"
44.           else
45.             echo "Encoded config: not provided"
46.           fi
47. 
48.       - name: Checkout repository
49.         uses: actions/checkout@v4
50.         with:
51.           submodules: true
52.           token: ${{ secrets.PAT_TOKEN || github.token }}
53. 
54.       - name: Set up Java 17
55.         uses: actions/setup-java@v4
56.         with:
57.           distribution: temurin
58.           java-version: "17"
59. 
60.       - name: Set up Node.js
61.         uses: actions/setup-node@v4
62.         with:
63.           node-version: "20"
64. 
65.       - name: Setup Flutter
66.         uses: subosito/flutter-action@v2
67.         with:
68.           flutter-version: '3.35.3'
69.           channel: stable
70.           cache: true
71. 
72.       - name: Export tool cache keys
73.         env:
74.           AI_TEAMMATE_CONFIG_FILE: ${{ inputs.config_file }}
75.           AI_TEAMMATE_CONCURRENCY_KEY: ${{ inputs.concurrency_key }}

 name: AI Teammate
 
 run-name: "${{ inputs.config_file }} : ${{ inputs.concurrency_key }}"
 
 on:
   workflow_dispatch:
     inputs:
       config_file:
         description: "Path to config"
         required: true
       encoded_config:
         description: "Encoded or JSON Agent Config"
         required: false
       concurrency_key:
         description: "Ticket key for concurrency grouping (e.g., TS-1)"
         required: true
       input_jql:
         description: "Optional Jira input JQL; defaults to key = concurrency_key"
         required: false
       project_key:
         description: "Project key for multi-project config selection"
         required: false
 
 permissions:
   contents: write
   pull-requests: write
   actions: read
 
 jobs:
   ai-teammate:
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
     concurrency:
       group: ai-teammate-${{ inputs.config_file }}-${{ inputs.concurrency_key }}
       cancel-in-progress: false
 
     steps:
       - name: Job info
         run: |
           echo "Config file: ${{ inputs.config_file }}"

           AI_TEAMMATE_CONFIG_FILE: ${{ inputs.config_file }}

         with:

           key: ${{ runner.os }}-npm-global-copilot-1.0.44
           restore-keys: |
             ${{ runner.os }}-npm-global-copilot-

1. name: AI Teammate
2. 
3. run-name: "${{ inputs.config_file }} : ${{ inputs.concurrency_key }}"
4. 
5. on:
6.   workflow_dispatch:
7.     inputs:
8.       config_file:
9.         description: "Path to config"
10.         required: true
11.       encoded_config:
12.         description: "Encoded or JSON Agent Config"
13.         required: false
14.       concurrency_key:
15.         description: "Ticket key for concurrency grouping (e.g., TS-1)"
16.         required: true
17.       input_jql:
18.         description: "Optional Jira input JQL; defaults to key = concurrency_key"
19.         required: false
20.       project_key:
21.         description: "Project key for multi-project config selection"
22.         required: false
23. 
24. permissions:
25.   contents: write
26.   pull-requests: write
27.   actions: read
28. 
29. jobs:
30.   ai-teammate:
31.     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
32.     concurrency:
33.       group: ai-teammate-${{ inputs.config_file }}-${{ inputs.concurrency_key }}
34.       cancel-in-progress: false
35. 
36.     steps:
37.       - name: Job info
38.         run: |
39.           echo "Config file: ${{ inputs.config_file }}"
40.           echo "Concurrency key: ${{ inputs.concurrency_key }}"
41.           echo "Display key: ${{ inputs.display_key || inputs.concurrency_key }}"
42.           if [ -n "${{ inputs.encoded_config }}" ]; then
43.             echo "Encoded config: provided"
44.           else
45.             echo "Encoded config: not provided"
46.           fi
47. 
48.       - name: Checkout repository
49.         uses: actions/checkout@v4
50.         with:
51.           submodules: true
52.           token: ${{ secrets.PAT_TOKEN || github.token }}
53. 
54.       - name: Set up Java 17
55.         uses: actions/setup-java@v4
56.         with:
57.           distribution: temurin
58.           java-version: "17"
59. 
60.       - name: Set up Node.js
61.         uses: actions/setup-node@v4
62.         with:
63.           node-version: "20"
64. 
65.       - name: Setup Flutter
66.         uses: subosito/flutter-action@v2
67.         with:
68.           flutter-version: '3.35.3'
69.           channel: stable
70.           cache: true
71. 
72.       - name: Export tool cache keys
73.         env:
74.           AI_TEAMMATE_CONFIG_FILE: ${{ inputs.config_file }}
75.           AI_TEAMMATE_CONCURRENCY_KEY: ${{ inputs.concurrency_key }}
76.           AI_TEAMMATE_DISPLAY_KEY: ${{ inputs.display_key || inputs.concurrency_key }}
77.         run: bash agents/setup/cache.sh keys dmtools:v1.7.196 codegraph copilot:1.0.44 copilot-session playwright
78. 
79.       - name: Cache Playwright browsers
80.         uses: actions/cache@v4
81.         with:
82.           path: ~/.cache/ms-playwright
83.           key: ${{ runner.os }}-playwright-browsers-${{ hashFiles('.github/workflows/ai-teammate.yml') }}
84.           restore-keys: |
85.             ${{ runner.os }}-playwright-browsers-
86. 
87.       - name: Install Playwright and Chromium
88.         run: |
89.           python3 -m pip install --upgrade pip
90.           python3 -m pip install playwright pytest
91.           python3 -m playwright install --with-deps chromium
92. 
93.       - name: Configure npm global prefix
94.         run: |
95.           npm config set prefix ~/.npm-global
96.           echo "$HOME/.npm-global/bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"
97.           echo "$HOME/.local/bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"
98. 
99.       - name: Cache npm global packages
100.         if: vars.AI_AGENT_PROVIDER == 'copilot'
101.         uses: actions/cache@v4
102.         with:
103.           path: |
104.             ~/.npm-global
105.             ~/.local/bin
106.           key: ${{ runner.os }}-npm-global-copilot-1.0.44
107.           restore-keys: |
108.             ${{ runner.os }}-npm-global-copilot-
109.             ${{ runner.os }}-npm-global-
110. 
111.       - name: Cache Copilot session
112.         if: vars.AI_AGENT_PROVIDER == 'copilot'
113.         uses: actions/cache@v4
114.         with:
115.           path: ${{ env.COPILOT_SESSION_CACHE_PATH }}
116.           key: ${{ env.COPILOT_SESSION_CACHE_KEY }}
117.           restore-keys: |
118.             ${{ env.COPILOT_SESSION_CACHE_RESTORE_KEY }}
119. 
120.       - name: Cache Copilot session
121.         if: vars.AI_AGENT_PROVIDER == 'copilot'
122.         uses: actions/cache@v4
123.         with:
124.           path: ${{ env.COPILOT_SESSION_CACHE_PATH }}
125.           key: ${{ env.COPILOT_SESSION_CACHE_KEY }}
126.           restore-keys: |
127.             ${{ env.COPILOT_SESSION_CACHE_RESTORE_KEY }}
128. 
129.       - name: Install Copilot CLI
130.         if: vars.AI_AGENT_PROVIDER == 'copilot'
131.         run: |
132.           if ! command -v copilot >/dev/null 2>&1; then
133.             npm install -g @github/copilot@1.0.44
134.           fi
135.           copilot --version
136.           echo "Copilot CLI ready"
137. 
138.       - name: Verify Copilot CLI installation
139.         if: vars.AI_AGENT_PROVIDER == 'copilot'
140.         env:
141.           COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN || secrets.PAT_TOKEN }}
142.         run: |
143.           copilot --version || echo "Copilot CLI will be available once credentials are configured"
144. 
145.       - name: Validate Copilot model
146.         if: vars.AI_AGENT_PROVIDER == 'copilot'
147.         env:
148.           COPILOT_MODEL: ${{ vars.COPILOT_MODEL }}
149.         run: |
150.           if [ -z "${COPILOT_MODEL}" ]; then
151.             echo "::error::COPILOT_MODEL repository variable must be set for copilot provider"
152.             exit 1
153.           fi
154.           echo "Using Copilot model: ${COPILOT_MODEL}"
155. 
156.       - name: Export tool cache keys
157.         run: bash agents/setup/cache.sh keys dmtools:v1.7.196 codegraph
158. 
159.       - name: Cache DMTools
160.         uses: actions/cache@v4
161.         with:
162.           path: ${{ env.DMTOOLS_CACHE_PATH }}
163.           key: ${{ env.DMTOOLS_CACHE_KEY }}
164.           restore-keys: |
165.             dmtools-
166. 
167.       - name: Cache CodeGraph binary
168.         uses: actions/cache@v4
169.         with:
170.           path: ${{ env.CODEGRAPH_CACHE_PATH }}
171.           key: ${{ env.CODEGRAPH_CACHE_KEY }}
172.           restore-keys: |
173.             ${{ runner.os }}-npm-global-codegraph-
174.             ${{ runner.os }}-npm-global-
175. 
176.       - name: Cache CodeGraph index
177.         uses: actions/cache@v4
178.         with:
179.           path: ${{ env.CODEGRAPH_INDEX_CACHE_PATH }}
180.           key: ${{ env.CODEGRAPH_INDEX_CACHE_KEY }}-${{ github.sha }}
181.           restore-keys: |
182.             ${{ env.CODEGRAPH_INDEX_CACHE_KEY }}-${{ github.ref_name }}-
183.             ${{ env.CODEGRAPH_INDEX_CACHE_KEY }}-
184. 
185.       - name: Install DMTools v1.7.196
186.         run: bash agents/setup/install.sh dmtools:v1.7.196
187. 
188.       - name: Install and init CodeGraph
189.         run: bash agents/setup/codegraph.sh
190. 
191.       - name: Install CodeGraph usage guard
192.         run: |
193.           real_codegraph="$(command -v codegraph)"
194.           guard_dir="${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}/.agent-bin"
195.           mkdir -p "${guard_dir}" "${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}/.dmtools"
196.           cat > "${guard_dir}/codegraph" <<EOF
197.           #!/usr/bin/env bash
198.           set -euo pipefail
199.           log="\${CODEGRAPH_USAGE_LOG:-${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}/.dmtools/codegraph-usage.log}"
200.           mkdir -p "\$(dirname "\${log}")"
201.           printf '%s\tcodegraph' "\$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" >> "\${log}"
202.           for arg in "\$@"; do
203.             printf ' %q' "\${arg}" >> "\${log}"
204.           done
205.           printf '\n' >> "\${log}"
206.           exec "${real_codegraph}" "\$@"
207.           EOF
208.           chmod +x "${guard_dir}/codegraph"
209.           echo "${guard_dir}" >> "${GITHUB_PATH}"
210.           echo "CODEGRAPH_USAGE_LOG=${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}/.dmtools/codegraph-usage.log" >> "${GITHUB_ENV}"
211. 
212.       - name: Configure Git author
213.         run: |
214.           git config --global user.name "trackstate-ai"
215.           git config --global user.email "trackstate-ai@users.noreply.github.com"
216. 
217.       - name: Prepare agent config
218.         env:
219.           RAW_ENCODED_CONFIG: ${{ inputs.encoded_config }}
220.         run: |
221.           node <<'NODE'
222.           const fs = require('fs');
223. 
224.           const raw = process.env.RAW_ENCODED_CONFIG || '';
225.           let config = {};
226. 
227.           if (raw.trim()) {
228.             const candidates = [raw];
229.             try {
230.               candidates.push(decodeURIComponent(raw));
231.             } catch (error) {
232.               // Keep raw-only candidates when the input is already JSON.
233.             }
234. 
235.             const parsed = candidates
236.               .map((candidate) => {
237.                 try { return JSON.parse(candidate); } catch (error) { return null; }
238.               })
239.               .find(Boolean);
240. 
241.             if (!parsed) {
242.               throw new Error('encoded_config is neither JSON nor URL-encoded JSON');
243.             }
244.             config = parsed;
245.           }
246. 
247.           config.params = config.params || {};
248.           const prompts = Array.isArray(config.params.cliPrompts)
249.             ? config.params.cliPrompts.slice()
250.             : [];
251.           ['./agents/prompts/bash_tools.md', './agents/prompts/codegraph_tools.md'].forEach((prompt) => {
252.             if (!prompts.includes(prompt)) prompts.push(prompt);
253.           });
254.           config.params.cliPrompts = prompts;
255. 
256.           fs.appendFileSync(process.env.GITHUB_ENV, `EFFECTIVE_ENCODED_CONFIG=${encodeURIComponent(JSON.stringify(config))}\n`);
257.           NODE
258. 
259.       - name: Run AI Teammate

265.           JIRA_AUTH_TYPE: ${{ vars.JIRA_AUTH_TYPE }}
266.           JIRA_TRANSFORM_CUSTOM_FIELDS_TO_NAMES: ${{ vars.JIRA_TRANSFORM_CUSTOM_FIELDS_TO_NAMES }}
267.           JIRA_EXTRA_FIELDS_PROJECT: ${{ vars.JIRA_EXTRA_FIELDS_PROJECT }}
268.           JIRA_EXTRA_FIELDS: ${{ vars.JIRA_EXTRA_FIELDS || '[]' }}
269.           JIRA_CLEAR_CACHE: "true"
270.           DMTOOLS_CACHE_ENABLED: ${{ vars.DMTOOLS_CACHE_ENABLED }}
271. 
272.           CONFLUENCE_EMAIL: ${{ secrets.JIRA_EMAIL }}
273.           CONFLUENCE_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN }}
274.           CONFLUENCE_BASE_PATH: ${{ vars.CONFLUENCE_BASE_PATH }}
275.           CONFLUENCE_DEFAULT_SPACE: ${{ vars.CONFLUENCE_DEFAULT_SPACE }}
276. 
277.           FIGMA_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.FIGMA_TOKEN }}
278.           FIGMA_BASE_PATH: ${{ vars.FIGMA_BASE_PATH }}
279. 
280.           PAT_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.PAT_TOKEN }}
281.           GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.PAT_TOKEN }}
282.           GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.PAT_TOKEN }}
283.           SOURCE_GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.PAT_TOKEN }}
284.           SOURCE_GITHUB_WORKSPACE: IstiN
285.           SOURCE_GITHUB_REPOSITORY: trackstate
286. 
287.           GEMINI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.GEMINI_API_KEY }}
288.           GEMINI_MODEL: ${{ vars.GEMINI_MODEL }}
289.           GEMINI_DEFAULT_MODEL: ${{ vars.GEMINI_DEFAULT_MODEL }}
290.           GEMINI_BASE_PATH: ${{ vars.GEMINI_BASE_PATH }}
291. 
292.           COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN || secrets.PAT_TOKEN }}
293.           COPILOT_MODEL: ${{ vars.COPILOT_MODEL }}
294.           AI_AGENT_PROVIDER: ${{ vars.AI_AGENT_PROVIDER }}
295.           AI_TEAMMATE_CONFIG_FILE: ${{ inputs.config_file }}

298. 
299.           DMTOOLS_INTEGRATIONS: "jira,confluence,figma,ai,cli,file,github"
300.           PROMPT_CHUNK_TOKEN_LIMIT: ${{ vars.PROMPT_CHUNK_TOKEN_LIMIT }}
301.           CLI_ALLOWED_COMMANDS: "find,ls,cat,mkdir,bash,git,gh,flutter,dart,python3,pytest,run-agent.sh,codegraph"
